action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 13 eval failed because predict.py caused CUDA OOM with batch_size=256 during ESM2 inference on the test set. The predict.py in the workspace is also truncated/incomplete. Best test spearman remains 0.6735 (iter 8). I need a complete, robust predict.py that uses a safe batch size (32) with periodic CUDA cache clearing, and correctly loads the new torch MLP checkpoint (checkpoints/mlp.pt) from iter 12. Analysis: ESM2-150M attention activations scale quadratically with sequence length (~737 tokens). Batch size 256 is far too large for 31.8 GB VRAM. Reducing to 32 should keep memory well under the limit while still finishing inference in ~15-20 minutes. I must also complete the truncated main() function and ensure WT sequence is loaded from the saved wt_info.pt since train.csv is unavailable during eval. Decision: Rewrite complete predict.py with batch_size=32, cache clearing, and full checkpoint loading logic. Run a quick syntax/import check, then immediately submit eval for checkpoints/mlp.pt.
